an3.1:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta's Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
an3.1:1.5: “Yes, lord,” the monks responded to him.
an3.1:2.1: “Monks, whatever dangers arise all arise from a fool, and not from a wise person.
an3.1:2.2: Whatever disasters arise all arise from a fool, and not from a wise person.
an3.1:2.3: Whatever troubles arise all arise from a fool, and not from a wise person.“
an3.1:2.4: Just as a fire, released from a house of reeds or a house of grass, burns even gabled houses, plastered inside & out, draft-free, with close-fitting doors & windows shut against the wind;
an3.1:2.5: in the same way, whatever dangers arise all arise from a fool, and not from a wise person.
an3.1:2.6: Whatever disasters arise all arise from a fool, and not from a wise person.
an3.1:2.7: Whatever troubles arise all arise from a fool and not from a wise person.
an3.1:3.1: “Thus, monks, a fool brings danger; a wise person brings no danger.
an3.1:3.2: A fool brings disasters; a wise person brings no disasters.
an3.1:3.3: A fool brings trouble; a wise person brings no trouble.
an3.1:3.4: “There is no danger from a wise person. There are no disasters from a wise person. There is no trouble from a wise person.
an3.1:4.2: ‘We will avoid the three things that, endowed with which, one is to be recognized as a fool. We will undertake & maintain the three things that, endowed with which, one is to be recognized as a wise person.’
an3.1:4.3: That’s how you should train yourselves.”
